body:
Title: CRA Form T1206 2025 Tax on Split Income.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t1206/t1206-25e.pdf

CRA T1206 2025 calculates tax on split income. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.33, 0.145, 0.090301, and 0.150198. Part 1 calculates TOSI-adjusted net income. Line 1 is the part of taxable dividends, eligible and other than eligible, from taxable Canadian corporations. Line 2 is the part of line 1 that is other-than-eligible dividends. Line 3 is all other split income. Line 4 adds line 1 and line 3, is total split income, and is entered on line 23200 and line 68360. Line 5 is net income from line 23600. Line 6 adds line 4 and line 5 and may be negative.

Part 2 calculates federal tax on split income. Line 8 multiplies total split income from line 7 by 33%, encoded as 0.33, and is entered on line 40424. Line 8A divides line 8 by 14.5%, encoded as 0.145. Line 10 adds line 8 and line 9. Line 13 adds federal disability amount line 31600 and transferred disability amount line 31800. Line 14 multiplies line 13 by 14.5%, encoded as 0.145. Line 15 subtracts line 14 from line 10. For the federal dividend tax credit on split income, line 16 is line 1 from Part 1, line 17 is line 2 from Part 1, line 18 is line 16 minus line 17 floored at zero, line 19 multiplies line 17 by 9.0301%, encoded as 0.090301, line 20 multiplies line 18 by 15.0198%, encoded as 0.150198, and line 21 adds line 19 and line 20. Line 22 subtracts line 21 from line 15. Line 23 prorates federal foreign tax credit line 40500 by split income from foreign sources line 68370 divided by total income from foreign sources line 68380. Line 24 subtracts line 23 from line 22 and floors the result at zero. Line 27 subtracts line 26 from line 25 and floors the result at zero. Line 29 adds line 27 and line 28. Line 30 is the greater of line 24 and line 29. The form adds line 30 to line 41800 and enters the result on line 42000.

Part 3 calculates provincial or territorial tax payable by an individual with split income when Form T2203 is not completed. Line 31 is total split income from line 4. Line 32 is the high tax rate from Chart C. Line 33 multiplies line 31 by line 32. Line 33C divides line 33A by the low tax rate from Chart C. Line 36 adds provincial or territorial disability tax credit lines 58440 and 58480. Line 38 multiplies line 36 by the low tax rate from Chart C. Line 39 subtracts line 38 from line 33. Lines 40 to 46 calculate provincial or territorial dividend tax credit on split income using the Chart C eligible and other-than-eligible dividend tax credit rates. Line 47 subtracts line 46 from line 39. Line 49 prorates the provincial or territorial foreign tax credit by split non-business foreign-source income divided by total non-business foreign-source income. Line 50 subtracts line 49 from line 47. Line 52 is the greater of line 50 and line 51 and is entered on line 42800.
